_metadata.description: Ophthalmology framework — eye anatomy, vision loss patterns, red eye DDx, major eye diseases.
_metadata.source: AAO Basic & Clinical Science Course; Wills Eye Manual

conjunctivitis.viral: watery discharge, often bilateral, contagious; adenovirus common
conjunctivitis.bacterial: purulent discharge; S. aureus, S. pneumoniae

red_eye_differential.keratitis_corneal_ulcer: pain, photophobia, white spot; bacterial/HSV/fungal/acanthamoeba (contact lens)
red_eye_differential.uveitis: deep pain, photophobia, hypopyon; associated with autoimmune (HLA-B27)
red_eye_differential.acute_angle_closure_glaucoma: severe pain, headache, nausea, halos around lights, fixed mid-dilated pupil — emergency
red_eye_differential.scleritis: deep boring pain, often associated with autoimmune (RA)

primary_open_angle.presentation: asymptomatic until late, peripheral vision loss
primary_open_angle.treatment: topical (PG analogs, β-blockers, α-agonists, CAIs); laser (SLT); surgery

age_related_macular_degeneration_AMD.dry_geographic: drusen, slow loss of central vision

diabetic_retinopathy.screening: annual dilated exam in all diabetics

optic_neuritis.presentation: subacute vision loss + pain on eye movement + dyschromatopsia

pupillary_abnormalities.afferent_pupillary_defect_APD_Marcus_Gun: optic nerve lesion; swinging flashlight test

ocular_emergencies: giant cell arteritis with vision loss
